{{DISPLAYTITLE:Republic of Aurielle}}

[[File:Auriellen Coat of Arms by Liminal (tysm).png|center|500px|link=]]

{{Quote|Nevi Aurielleno Onem Quiusen}}

==Description==
{{Simple Image Block
|image= Republic of Aurielle New.png
|caption=Flag of the Republic of Aurielle.}}
'''Aurielle''', officially the '''Republic of Aurielle''', is an international self-proclaimed nations found in a variety of levels within the Backrooms. Its population stands between 72,000 and 76,934, making it one of the largest groups, and sizable numbers of its population are concentrated in Volucris, the group's capital and largest city, followed by Valais, Prospero, and Seileta. Aurielle's official and most spoken language is [[Aurielleno]], but English and other languages are commonplace.

Aurielle dates its political inception to the Franco-Italian Mirave communities in [[Level 10]] and the Abamastor city-states, its divergent sisters in [[Level 40]]. Antione Anselmo, leader of the city of Mirves, enacted a program of centralization that inadvertently allowed the creation of Creole Aurielleno and served as an initiator to the goal of Auriellen unification. By 1908 the size of the states in Abamastor forced the expedition of the Four Quarter Expeditions and its subsequent Gande Mivacao to the Elvione corridor, where passing missionaries belonging to the Church of Etrial brought their practices and established the Église d'Chassenon. The modern predecessor of Aurielle was established on October 19, 1921, with the Declayansiya de Furmazireba, which proclaimed the Principality of Persa Uisa. Its leader, Sepp Sunil, ordained Elvione unification. Ander Sunil became prince following Sepp's death, and Persa reached its influential and territorial epitome under Sunil but also faced hardships such as the LOTG Disaster and the Ciran Wars. Persa was ultimately disbanded after Silas Ander's resignation and the subsequent schism to take the throne. A period of nomadic endeavors continued, characterized by internal strife to reunify Persa, till 1961 when Halnora was formed. Creole Aurielleno transformed into Aurielleno, and the nation entered a unique era of technological, trade, and scientific greatness, which established the nation as a regional power within the political landscape. Halnora collapsed after the Alsanium Uprising, marking the beginning of a second period of chaotic division before the Istauran Federation embarked on a unification effort. Ultimately, the expansionist policies of the federation marked its end as a coalition in the Muran Conflict took it over. Remnants of the Istauran government relocated and became part of the [[Backroom Colonists]], where they established a colony in [[Level 479]] for a brief period of time before an abrupt failure cutting communication stations with central command left them to leave the colony. The former Istauran leaders stumbled upon Arisa in [[Level 339]] and eventually won the elections, which would launch the Vistokan Wars.

The Republic of Aurielle was officially formed in 1975 with the Common Unity Act. The early Auriellen state, especially under Bles Caida, was devoted to rebuilding cities and towns damaged during the Vistokan Wars, as well as revitalizing areas long neglected by the previous regimes. In 1984, tensions between Antlian locals and the Auriellens exploded into the Antlian Revolution, which set forth a period of increased militarism and civil rights backsliding under the far-right Sebalente Solcito. The Netter Linsho War, which led to the annexation of Antlian lands, brought Solcito into direct conflict with Antlian integrationists, who threatened rearmament, and heightened opposition by the coalition, which had begun to increasingly push towards impeachment. He was forced into de-radicalization and began the Hylmaren Plan, preceding the events of the AAC incident. The Lustruger Crescen War, caused by the AAC Incident, led to the nearly ten-year occupation of Auriellen lands by MEG, Antlia, and the Confederation of Level 154 and a state of near-perpetual civil war by 1992. A 19-year conflict for the liberation of Aurielle was launched and reinstated Aurielle with additional territorial gains, a new constitution, and new roles. Aurielle became a key founding member of the Volucris Strategic Economic and Security Accord. Arenet Matlon's election as president began a shift in foreign policy that saw Aurielle actively involved in foreign conflicts, expanding Aurielle's sphere of influence.

A developed country with an advanced economy, Aurielle is a federal state with some of the largest amount of GDP, the largest defense spending, and one of the largest manufacturing industries in the Backrooms, and it plays a significant role in international affairs due to its position as a superpower. Aurielle is a founding member and de facto leader of the VSESA, alongside numerous other international organizations and forums. They have a considerable agricultural and technological sector, especially in military systems.

==Etymology==
The name of Aurielle is frequently interpreted as being a combination of the [[Aurielleno]] words 'gold' and 'land' (aurum and limin, respectively) with some adjustments. This is thought to be inherited from the golden lands of [[Level 339]], where Volucris resides. Another interpretation is that the name Aurielle means "country" or "unity" in Creole Aurielleno (cauvellem).

==History==
===Pre-Persan===
Prior to the political formation of Persa Uisa, the northern Franco-Italian Mirave communities in [[Level 10]] diverged from their counterparts found in the Nasate River and accidentally landed in [[Level 40]], where they evolved into the Abamastor city-states. Antione Anselmo, leader of the city of Mirves, enacted a program of unilateral centralization with the goal of transitioning from city states to a monarchal polity. Despite the failures of Anselmo to embroider a united Abamastor, his efforts allowed for the creation of Creole Aurielleno and served as a initiator to the goal of Auriellen unification observed throughout the years.

As the population of the cities within Abamastor grew and naturally transitioned into established political entities, food became an increasingly scarce resource. By 1908, Mirves, Valmari, and Varmiyan accelerated the Four Quarter Expeditions to Level 10, aiming to reestablish contact with the Miravean community. While its goal was a failure, mainly due to the technological limitations restricting travel at the time, the expedition caused the Gande Mivacao (Grand Migration) that triggered population loss and political uncertainty but assisted in trade and allowed for the further growth of the states. Following the establishment of the Elvione corridor, passing missionaries belonging to the Church of Etrial brought their practices and thoughts into the land and later on built the Église d'Chassenon with assistance from the Celsarans.

Interest in exploration grew as Valmarian explorer Edmondo Hérrison, in a scouting mission to locate civilization outside of Elvione, discovered [[Level 339]] and established a temporary outpost there. The Elvione population nearly doubled, and advancements in medicine and other fields improved living conditions significantly.

===Early History===
The Principality of Persa Uisa emerged on October 19, 1921, out of the transition from interim government to principality in Persevusa according to the Declayansiya de Furmazireba (Declaration of Formation, Creole Aurielleno). From the beginning, Persa Uisa was defined by the centralization of power by the hands of its early princes, the fervent cultivation of a distinct cultural identity, and the embracement of expansionism and exploration as core tenets of the state. It is often referred to as the primary foundational block of the Auriellen state's culture and practices, many of which continue on to this day.

During its early years, Sepp Sunil became entangled with the vision of Elvioneian unification, based on Anselmo's unified Abamastor, and subsequently began diplomatic talks with surrounding states. In his 1921 "Speech to the Harmonious Lands," Sepp outlined that unification would put Elvione in a better position than if it were separated into distinct territories and mentioned the shared past and conjoined cultural identities of the citizens of Elvione. Through a vote in early 1922, the different nations, except the Church of Etrial, agreed to join Persa Uisa.

After its unification, Persa Uisa steadily expanded, albeit logistics continuously thwarted their advances. Despite heightened public enthusiasm for exploration and territorial expansion, Sepp Sunil ordered the controversial and abrupt stop to further exploration missions, to the dismay of his advisors and the general public, in order to improve technologies and better living conditions. Protests and rallies in opposition to the change happened abroad, which led to the resumption of operations as public pressure and advisorial guidance mounted. After the controversy, a modification to the constitution was ratified, which furthered constitutionalization and limited the powers of the prince.

After Sepp died in 1924 due to natural causes, Ander Sunil was coronated as the new prince. Under his leadership, who has earned the endearing nickname of the Covuifub di Aurielle (Savior of Aurielle) among the general populace and historians alike, Persa reached its influential and territorial epitome. Ander drafted and implemented various acts aimed at improving education and developing a skilled workforce to facilitate the transition to industrialization. At the same time, he sought to realign colonial priorities to more utilitarian levels and to engage with the international community while avoiding sanctions. During this period of rampant expansionism, Persan explorer Marcus Agosti discovered Level 107 on March 12th, 1926, and both civilizations began trading, leading to the start of the long Auriellen-Cascadan alliance. Yet, the imperial ambitions of Ander came to an end in the late 1930s with the death of renowed Persan military leader Linan Maitre and the LOTG Communications Disaster, wherein several domestically produced LOTG MA2 Deveceo Komyunonia failed or spontaneously combusted. The Raiders, a now-defunct group based in [[Level 1]], attacked a Persan colony in the level, inciting the Ciran Wars.

The Ciran Wars were comparably diminutive to the future conflicts that would plague its predecessors; however, it determined the fate of the formation and trajectory of Aurielle. Three conflicts incorporate the Ciran Wars, the second being the most brutal. The push towards the Persan capital, Eáusig, by the Raiders led to the formation of the Nomino Daestory, whereby Ander Sunil survived 21 minutes of constant Raider attack and formed a counterattack to reunite with Persan reinforecements in the west. While the story has been denoted as hyperbole and inaccurate, it has entrenched itself as a popular myth and has been reciprocated in both popular slogans and propaganda posters. By the Treaty of Menastor signed in 1940, Persa emerged victorious and had mostly destroyed the Raider's capabilities. Ander Sunil would later die a national hero from the [[Hydrolitis Plague]] in 1942.

The death of Ander left the principality in the hands of Silas Ander, who proved less able to sustain its empire due to his lack of refined knowledge in international and domestic politics. In June 1942, the first Persan colony was disbanded as a result of logistical mismanagement, marking the beginning of the Persan decline. Opinion regarding his rule remained indecisive as the effects of the LOTG communications disaster hindered the flow of information, and communication continued to gradually be reopened. Throughout the following weeks, Silas attempted to impose tax reforms and tariffs to stimulate economic growth; however, most were stopped by the Parliament. Throughout the following weeks, major external territories were forced into complete autarky, leading to widespread unrest and organized protests against government inaction. Under heavy public scrutiny, Silas announced his resignation.

The system of the Persa Uisa constitutionally forbade the resignation of a prince, but Silas ran away. With a lack of a substitute, the Parliament ordered an emergency meeting, but by then it had been too late, as a schism to seize power led to a diaspora and various armed uprisings by opportunists in outer territories.

===Nomadic Period===
After the schism and subsequent collapse of Persa, the former territories of the principality fragmented into a mosaic of independent states. While most settlements now governed themselves, a majority of the population effectively became nomads wandering about. Over the following months, regional centers of power attempted to assert their influence and reunify Persa, the most successful of whom was the Commune of Independent Persan States (CIPS), established by the National Parliament in the Elvione area, which became wealthy through the trade of wheat and worms to surrounding states.

The unitary commune consolidated a vast amount of previously Persan territories after centralizing and fully organizing the state's military. Despite these successes, the expansionist efforts of CIPS were abruptly halted by the Raiders, who sought revenge and drew them back into a continuation of the Ciran Wars. The considerably weaker military of the commune led to its capitulation and disbandment in 1949 after the capture of Brevanavis; most civilians fled to avoid persecution by the Raiders; however, others stayed behind to engage in asymmetrical warfare.

Meanwhile, other remnants persisted elsewhere and attempted to capitalize on the faltering CIPS. They periodically launched raids and assumed control of colonial holdings, while also claiming themselves as rightful successors to the Persan crown. Despite their attempts at reunification and realignment, most of these holdouts naturally withered away or were defeated by foreign powers and disbanded in their conclusion.

===Halnora===
The defeat of CIPS catalyzed the beginning of a long vacuum in regard to polities willing to take on Persan reunification. Halnora was founded in 1961 in the wake of a constitutional convention of paramilitary leaders that vowed to create a free, successor state to the Persa Uisa. Arde Nesserman, who assumed control after being appointed by the Federal National Social Party as the first Federecidium, defined the cultural and political values and roles of the Halnoran state, most still present in modern times. Through Nesserman's reforms, the Creole Aurielleno language concluded a decades-long semantics shift officially transforming it into contemporary [[Aurielleno]], and placed the cultural values of respect and unity as paramount.

Through most of Nesserman's tenure, the nation experienced a golden age that lasted until the Alsanium Uprising in late 1966. Halnora settled itself as a regional power in the levels they inhabited and had gained huge capital wealth through their technology, trade, and scientific innovation, which had grown to prominence throughout the Backrooms for their versatility and longevity. Despite the potential for expansion beyond their borders, Nesserman was known for his isolationist policy and inconsistent, sporadic engagement with the international community. The 1962 Cultural Flourishing gave rise to Struvenism, characterized by the representation of peace and beauty in randomness. The efforts of Lorene Palem led to the popularization of the movement, which later developed into styles such as Arvusnoism, Culconism, and Paleno Art.
{{Simple Image Block
|image=Alsanium.png
|caption=Alsanium rebels arming themselves.
|align=left}}
Alsanium nationalism rose in 1963 due to increasing separation between their identity and those of mainland Halnora. In a speech by Kadyê Rashtenam detailing the government's ignorance to Alsanium and its identity, he set forth a plan to hold a unilateral referrendum about whether or not the province should separate from Halnora and declare independence. Diplomatic envoys were sent to address the issues; however, by then the locals saw the response as shallow and began arming themselves in preparation for an impending Halnoran military response. A significant turning point took place in mid-1966 when a Halnoran Self-Defense Force outpost located in Alsanium was ambushed by Alsaniumites in the area known as Freucracia; prior to this, Halnora continued to pursue diplomacy but felt vindicated of such responsibility following the ambush.

Halnora retained a defense and guard force that were largely unfit for large confrontations due to a policy of disengagement. Loyalists within Alsanium were the first to respond to the province's separation by entrenching themselves in vital points while awaiting government reinforcements, who introduced partial conscription. The Halnoran Self-Defense Force (HSDF) used counterinsurgency tactics and skirmishing to exhaust the enemy forces but faced difficulty as it became evident that Halnora was not prepared for war and wasn't equipped to deal with the humid environment of [[Level 427]]. As fighting stalled to a deadlock, Halnora made the critical error of allocating further forces to fight in the Alsanium Uprising than necessary, leaving many colonies undefended and allowing for crime and raids to take place, ultimately collapsing the group.

In an attempt to recuperate order within Halnora, deployed troops were ordered to retreat and head back to their respective settlements. Alsanium declared victory and the Alsanium State was formed, which would later be renamed to Antlia, officialized with the Treaty of Sarnelle.

===Istauran Federation===
{{Simple Image Block
|image=Need a hand.png
|caption=Istauran soldiers in defensive positions.}}
Nuche Renahmen. The minister of military affairs seized the opportunity and couped the governors of the remaining Halnoran states to form the Istauran Federation. Under Renahmen, the military junta mandated the centralization of the provinces, accompanied by widespread censorship, and the seizure of the headquarters of several media companies operating within the country by the military. The regime passed a mandatory military service bill and orchestrated various military campaigns against competing states that, too, emerged from Halnora's collapse.

In 1967, the Federation launched sweeping reforms that entailed self-sufficiency and purged foreign influence from the state. The continued expansionism of the federation brought heightened tensions and made it the enemy of various nations. This precipitated the brief but intense Muran Conflict, wherein a coalition composed of the opposition of Istauran expansionism fought against the federation. The coalition fought against the federal army in its territory, though it nonetheless managed to push it back and annex major territories. The Federal Army remained on the defensive up until a botched autumn counteroffensive plan led to the collapse of Istauran lines and the surrender of the nation with the Treaty of Volatirrae.

While a majority of Istauran senior leadership committed suicide or were killed in the Murun Trials, some managed to avoid capture and relocated to the [[Backroom Colonists]] under new aliases, where they would become part of an expeditionary team that later set up base in [[Level 479]]. Dissatisfied with their circumstances, they initially planned to rebel but saw it as futile and accepted it until an abrupt communication failure between their base and central command, among other factors, led to the base's abandoment.

===Arisa and Vistokan Wars===
Arisa was established in [[Level 339]] on the modern-day lands of Volucris and traced back to the collapse of Halnora. Originally assuming other responsibilities, the arrival of the remaining Istauran leaders and their subsequent victory in the elections saw a shift in their goals towards those of reunification. Led by Levis Viquelo, who, though ideologically aligned with the Istauran faction, disagreed with many of their policies, primarily those of mass surveillence and dictatorship, enacted mass reforms in preparation for war. In 1969, Arisan forces crossed into Canpei and Recula with the objective to secure their lands as fast as possible before any proper military response could be mounted. Following the fall of Canpei, Recula capitulated the day after and was integrated into Arisan territory.

Arisa continued their campaigns, now focusing on Mehristea. The Mehristean Campaign lasted four months; Arisan forces pushed along Mehristea lands in Level 339 relatively quickly, and when they were forced to retreat, they followed them into [[Level 416]] and [[Level 16]], where they concentrated most of their land. The Battle of Cathash would be engraved into history as the first major deployment of vehicles in support of infantry, though the burden of transportation and fuel consumption meant that their use was limited all around. When Arisan forces poured into Level 416 and Level 16, they were confronted by the Wall of Kaphelia, a set of fortifications throughout Mehristea's capital and major population centers.
{{Simple Image Block
|image=Hello maria im off to korea.png
|caption=Light transportation vehicles passing by bushery.}}
The defense through Kaphelia held off further Arisan offensive operations, but Arisan military leaders opted to siege the bases while reservists continued their advances elsewhere. Arisan troops surrounding Kaphelia entrenched themselves and only attacked in brief, spontaneous skirmishes to further exhaust Mehristea's defenses. The campaign was extended through stagnation; no real fighting took place, nor did major military operations occur, as Arisan troops spent most time searching and locating Mehristean bases. Following the end of the Phoney War period, marked by the occupation of all Mehristean territories outside of Kaphelia's defenses, plans to breach the walls had begun drafting.

In the end, General Beyme Messer's Slingshot Plan was chosen. In Level 16, operations began with constant shelling following an easy breach, caused due to the siege wearing off the enemy's morale and resistance. In Level 416, fighting was harder than anticipated. After a discussion with his advisors, Messer was convinced and allowed forces to begin setting ablaze the surrounding forest in order to irreparably destroy the enemy's morale. The event came to be known as Apollo's Rain, and it was reasoned that the forests were being used by the enemy and thus eligible military targets. The plan succeeded but burned a large portion of controlled territory.

After the Mehristean Campaign, Viquelo created a post-war economic plan and ramped up military production, although factories producing outdated equipment were converted to civilian industrial facilities. Furthermore, the government began reconstruction through Canpei, Recula, and Mehristea to incentivize civil integration and deter armed resistance against the Arisa occupiers and opened up a reconstruction bond program that was used to fund efforts in areas affected by Apollo's Rain. Arisa improved roads and created logistical corridors for both military and civilian use. This peaceful period was shortlived as three nations, Altair, Naos, and Lusfeor formed an alliance and preemptively striked Arisa in fears of a future invasion.

Successes following the initial strikes were short-lived as the strike quickly subdued to a bloody stalemate. Both sides entrenched themselves in kilometers of defensive positions, and the would-be frontline was a silent, broken landscape only broken by the occasional skirmishes. As the war dragged on for several months, Alioth mobilized troops and launched a surprise and decisive invasion into Lusfeor and Altair. Alioth continued to encircle and break apart pockets of alliance troops, largely assisted by the Arisans, who kept the Lusfeorites, Altairans, and Naosians preoccupied. Following the Altairan capital's fall by the hands of Alioth, the remaining state's troops split their forces to address them.

Arisa launched an offensive against Aneos and Lusfeor as they had become largely undermanned. They aimed to meet up with Alioth troops for the possibility of a collaboration, which succeeded a few weeks later as they met in Lagroba. After the campaign had concluded, Arisa and Alioth opened formal diplomatic ties under the Treaty of Klesko, which brought the two nations militarily closer. As partisan activities got worse, both groups agreed that a military alliance would help them control the areas they had taken and prevent other invasions; this led to the creation of Sidus.

The initial undertakings of Sidus were primarily focused on pacification and breaking apart partisan activity. Under a proponent of "peaceful reintegration," agitators and anyone related to them were placed in reeducation camps and propagandized as "new birth" citizens who, despite their dissident activities, turned to trust the government. Sidus then focused on Cercea, Bisko, and numerous smaller factions that emerged following Halnora's collapse. These factions were invaded and ultimately annexed during the Golden Southern Campaign. Crux Sotorna, recognized as a major center for Abrahamic faiths, was not invaded and instead became an honorary member. The factions annexed underwent reorganizations divided between civil and industrial coagulations. 

The post-Golden Southern Campaign landscape saw increased tensions wherein widespread anti-occupation protests and riots occurred, prompting the alliance to deploy artillery and soldiers to disperse the crowds. The Biskoan National Liberation Army in Bisko began armed rebellion against the federal government, which captured towns and brought world attention. The armed conflict was short-lived and continues as a non-violent opposition movement against centralization and authoritarian rule. Nonetheless, Sidus shifted their focus towards bigger targets, electing Khandria and Antares because both had been engaging in a prolonged conflict for the past year.

The Sidusan invasion of both groups began in 1971. The 1st, 4th, and 7th SRF Companies spearheaded attacks into Khandrian colonies located in [[Level 156]], where they cut communications with central bases to stall a military response. Leateu Tanger had drafted plans to cut Antarean territories in half as they received a contact from the Antareans asking to join Sidus. While some within the Sidus Supreme Allied Commanders Headquarters opposed the decision to allow Antares into the alliance, a majority of officers argued that allowing them to join would mitigate the concern of pacification and invading their land. Ultimately, the vote was a landslide, where a majority agreed to let the nation into Sidus. Antares relocated troops to assist Sidusan forces, as underlined by their agreement, despite the chance of the frontline collapsing.

As Khandria rerouted forces to disrupt the Sidusan advances, Antarean forces exploited the disorganized defenses and began pushing through the largely undefended Khandrian Paulasta region and encircled major outposts. Meanwhile, the full momentum of the Sidusan invasion cut through the Khandrian heartland and divided the nation into two zones. By mid-1971, Khandria grew desperate and began throwing all it could to reunite with its eastern counterparts. Despite their efforts, Khandria's government agreed to capitulate on August 9, 1971, to the disappointment of some nationalistic military leaders, who attempted to continue their operations. But by then, most soldiers had grown tired, and the insurrection naturally fizzled.

As the Khandrians were defeated, the apparent threat of Sidus led the remaining nations to discuss the possibility of a counterweight to oppose their expansionism. Talks elongated, and the alliance planned to encompass the remaining states that had appeared from Halnira's collapse. Although the efforts brought the remaining nations closer, their states' own goals and ideologies, which often conflicted and included Halniran reunification, undermined their legitimacy. Sidusan spies intercepted and infiltrated these conferences, reporting them back, fueling the ambitions of Sidus, as they saw it as quintessential to take as much territory as possible before the conceptualization of their counter-alliance. Antares proposed a direct invasion of Earra and Valnitak, the two groups leading the effort.

The plan was shelved, as it had been considered too extreme, but it served as a gateway to the embarking of Operation Mumus Dumus. The plan aimed to significantly reduce the popularity of the ruling party in Valnitak in order to establish a government that would be friendly to Sidus. The Antareans, with endorsement from their allies, began a series of internal sabotages, political disruptions, and fabrication of scandals with the goal of staining their image and allowing the pro-Sidus Valnitak Civic Alliance to capitalize and win the election. From the economic turmoil and scandals caused by the Antareans, the VCA won and restructured the system to secure multiple supermajorities. In a late 1972 referendum, the nation would join Sidus.

As Valnitak was secured, Sidus set out to finalize its campaign in a final, ambitious offensive to bring about the end of the Vistokan Wars. Reeling from the loss of Valnitak, the newly formed "Lepus Emergency Alliance" (LEA) was the target of it. By January 26, 1973, all members of Sidus had organized their armies and were prepared for war. February 12th saw Sidusan forces make their landfall across Laniaken, Sophari, Urcea, and Rodhinea. By February 14th, the alliance achieved a rapid and decisive takeover of the smaller, isolated colonies of the opposing nations, effectively transforming them into forward operating bases set for future attacks.

Operation Labrys began on May 11th, headed by the 81st Light Infantry Brigade and 2nd Battalion, witnessing a push for the Laniaken city of Normare. As they advanced, other elements applied pressure to Sophari and Urcean military positions. The LeA led Sidus to assume more aggressive operations and ordered forces to seize as much territory before reinforcements arrived or before proper defensive lines could be mounted. By July 2nd, Sidus crept dangerously close to major territories in Sophar and Laniake, even managing to successfully capture Normare as the city's defenses faltered.
{{Simple Image Block
|image=Para para troppers.png
|caption=Antarean forces in the Urcean range.
|align=left}}
Onward, the campaign grew stagnant and bloody, catalyzed by attritional warfare. Neither side made significant gains, and by October 6th, it remained stagnant longer than anticipated, which forced Sidusan leadership to rethink their strategy. A strategy of "decapitation," which focused on the assasination of the LEA leadership and their internal structure, was formulated under Operation Long Knife. On October 1st, special forces elements from Antares and Arisa infiltrated the ranks of the LEA and its populace to perpetrate acts of political assassination and instill uprisings among sympathizers and collaborationist organizations. In spite of the operation, LEA troops eventually overwhelmed Sidusan defensive positions and pushed them back to defensive positions along Normare and Darview. The LEA launched a counteroffensive, spearheaded by the Sophari Republican Guard and supported by contingents from various nations. They made an inward bulge in the Sidusan lines as they made advances beyond the Sidusan lines.

In response, Sidus members relocated further funds into Operation Long Knife, began the usage of car bombs and other armored vehicles, employed psychological warfare, and ordered forces in Normare and Darview to pressure LEA in the south and north, respectively. Defensive positions alongside the 86th Hardleng and 74th Kelton stalled LEA troops by ambushing them. By January 21st, agents operating in Laniake successfully assassinated Laniakean president, Piolke Cinem, and led a Sidus-sponsored armed insurrection that attempted to seize leadership. The LEA response was sluggish, as they had hardly any remaining units to assist the Laniakean, though Antlia and Icaz were able to send reserves in support of the loyalist government.

Observing an opportunity, one Arisan, Alioth, and Antarean battalion each were rerouted to fight in Laniakea. Laniake was forced to pull out forces from the offensive to commit to defending its territories. While Laniakean forces were headed back, Sidus troops made quick work in seizing much of the capital and key governmental institutions, where they set up a puppet government. Caught between collapsing their advantageous position or rerouting forces to assist the Laniakeans, the LEA decided to fund anti-occupation organizations; however, these were intercepted and used to fund the puppet government and Sidus operations.

Additional troops were committed towards Laniake as Sidus initiated their incursion into other LEA territories, perpetrating an incursion that advanced them through Earra, Sophar, and Urcea. LEA began pulling out forces from the indent, leaving undermanned positions to hold out until reinforcements could be sent. Sidus forces began a counterattack at the indent while flanking forces continued their advances, seemingly forming an encirclement where a majority of LEA forces were. After the fall of Fyreland, Sidus' renewed offensive was thorough in ensuring encircled LEA troop pockets were utterly destroyed. Panic erupted among the LEA populations as Arisan, Alioth, and Antarean forces quickly advanced through their territory.

As the Mustacan Direction collapsed, it became, without a shadow of doubt, that the fall of LEA was inevitable. Sidus swiftly seized Sophar and Earra, and by August, a majority of the alliance's members capitulated or were taken over, leaving Antlia and Narda as the remnants of the opposition. Sidusan battle doctrine by then has specifically emphasized assured victory and a reduction of the loss of life. Indiscriminate artillery barrages pelted their territories, and the Antlians and Nardans were devastated, forcing the Nardans to capitulate. Once more, the Antlians were left alone; driven by nationalistic senses and hatred towards their deemed occupiers, they fought bitterly against the consequent Sidusan offensive. Their efforts, however, were overshadowed by the combined military strength of the alliance, ultimately leading to the conclusion of the Vistokan Wars on November 12th.

===Post-Vistokan Wars===
The Final Act of National Reunification, as it would be known by Auriellen historians, was by far the deadliest phase of the Vistokan Wars. Dozens of cities and bases were devastated, and thousands were dead. Arisa, the main perpetrator of the war, annexed most of the territories and received most of the amenities. Territorial changes followed soon after the finalization of the war, and an extensive reconstruction effort began. Arisan leadership continued towards the goal of Halnoran reinstatement and called for the alliance to unify under one state. The Inisiartus Comuc Fiuderale (Common Federation Initiative), as it became known, was set out by the largely unionist Arisan populace, with the collaboration of Antares and the other states.

Under the ICF, the creation of the Preparatory Agency for the Common People mandated an ambitious economic, social, and governmental standardization process. Despite challenges with leadership and ethnolinguistic differences, on 27 January 1975, the Common Unity Act was passed, formally establishing the Republic of Aurielle. Under a federal system of governance, which assured equal autonomy to all comprising states, a provisional government was put in place for state building and a transition to democracy.

===Republic of Aurielle===
Most of Bles Caida's administration was devoted to rebuilding cities and towns damaged during the Vistokan Wars, as well as revitalizing areas long neglected by the previous regimes. Much of the policy during this period focused on defining the functions of the new state, roles, and responsibilities that had not been clearly outlined in the newly ratified constitution. The local elections of 1975 gave way to the formation of additional political parties, including the Eperet Worepatores Socialis Forume Auriellenos, Asoverband deo 10s, and Nirumun et Libreta, who later became instrumental in the formation of a comprehensive reconstruction strategy, following those drafted by EWSFA. Paramet demilitarized the Republic in order to allocate resources elsewhere. At the same time, the government reorganized Sidus' intelligence community into the [[Auriellen Security Council]], formed due to fears of resurgent nationalistic groups spreading to regain independence.

Auriellen diplomacy shifted focus towards opening up Aurielle to receive assistance for reconstruction efforts through a broader investment initiative. Elements of the Auriellen Ground Forces aided the [[Duchy of Cascada]]'s Republican faction in the Tongramay Campaign, while also deploying troops to assist in Nayeong, Henghou, and along the Kenjaban-Dongsan perimeter to procure an allyship with the Cascadans despite the demilitarization. After agreements with Chekim and Vadella, Aurielle became a member of the Association of Enkeli States, and they furthered their international status by establishing relations with Branuba, Veridia, Arva, Hadrina, and Resa’adh. The transitional government transformed into the modern Auriellen Federal Government by 1980.

President Warin Paramet, who assumed power in 1980, continued reconstruction but maintained a much more lenient internal and foreign policy, allowing for greater decentralization. By 1983, reconstruction had mostly finalized in its greatest affected areas, even including Narda. Antlia was left as the final hurdle, and the government ordered a strategic and temporary relocation of civilians within Antlia to refugee centers until reconstruction services finished efforts. A nascent movement began to emerge in Antlia's south, particularly around Patuash, who, through organized protests and blockades, blocked off Auriellen services from entering the area until an agreement allowing Antlians to remain in their land was passed. As tensions between the Antlians and Auriellens escalated, opportunistic Natem Syafanasy seized the moment in 1984 and rallied the locals, leading to an independence uprising known as the Antlian Revolution.
{{Simple Image Block
|image=Ey im walking here.png
|caption=Auriellen forces boarding a helicopter during the Antlian Revolution.
|align=left}}
The Auriellen Ground Forces were sent to regain control over Antlia. However, they were defeated by Antlian partisans and paramilitary sympathizers, a total contrast to the Auriellen intervention in Tolistan a year prior. Fighting ceased on 16 November 1985 after the signing of the Járembare Agreement. Antlians held the support of the citizens of previous LEA states, though this would be put to the test due to their employment of terror tactics.

The failures of Paramet in the Antlia Revolution alienated the right-wing population, as they saw his mishandling of the crisis "as an attempt against Auriellen unity." The far-right capitalized on these events, promising swift changes that would reinvigorate the union of the people and demolish any separatist movement, which resonated with the disillusioned right-wing population seeking stability and unity in the wake of Paramet's failures. Wus dem Camvados's candidate, Sebalente Solcito, was elected, however, his party failed to reach a supermajority, as they had anticipated, in Congress due to the emergence of the EFU-AWSFA-Asover coalition.

Under Solcito, Aurielle experienced an era of centralization and the backsliding of civil rights. Solcito, who was inspired by Nuche Renahmen, attempted to seize media headquarters but was consistently blocked by the [[Aurielo Patoichism|neo-Patoichist]] coalition. In 1986, his regime passed the Ant Laws by 3 votes, beginning institutional segregation against Antlians. Entire communities were deported and their assets confiscated. Those who lacked foreign diplomatic protection were forcibly placed on mock-trials and exiled, most left to die. The far-right attempted to frame the Patoichist as Antlian sympathizers and expel them, but the Constitutional Court obstructed these efforts.

Solcito passed the Securitate Redcorsi Laupe Enacta. <ref>Security Reformation Bill Act</ref> that created the Auriellen Security Council Intelligence Center (ASCIC) and Auriellen Security Council Special Forces (ASCSF). The establishing of the Theory of Alignment brought Solcito's Aurielle closer to Neshatra under Ziódłkow and Dublisure, both of which were under the directive of hardline revolutionary leaders. Additionally, he endowed himself with the responsibility of the remilitarization of Aurielle, and authorized military presence in the peripherals of Antlia settlements, leading to skirmishes that would snowball into the Netter Linsho War. The remilitarization of Aurielle proved desirable in the ensuing conflict. Military operations ceased on April 3rd after the signing of the Treaty of Vanamarush, forcing Antlia to cede the Hadhidjara regions.

The conclusion of the Netter Linsho War increased Solcito's image but brought them in direct conflict with Antlian integrationists, who threatened rearmament, and heightened opposition by the coalition, which had begun to increasingly push towards impeachment. These conditions forced Solcito into a period of de-radicalization, beginning with the split of the scientific Auriellen community under Rama Saom from the increasingly militarized Republic. In line with this, Solcito dropped the Theory of Alignment and embarked on the Hylmaren Plan, a 2-year campaign to establish Aurielle as a great power on the global stage and as a strategic deterrent.

Under the terms of the new foreign policy, Aurielle established diplomatic relations with Hadrina, Storatrad, Kivennera, and most importantly, the [[M.E.G.]] Aurielle engaged in trade with the MEG, most importantly ammunition and weapons, during the Bloodbath of Cluster 1. Having observed the vitality of the Auriellen ammunition and arms industry, the MEG devised a plan to force a free trade agreement, beginning with the suspension of diplomatic relations and a subsequent accusation that stated that Aurielle was scamming them by sabotaging their equipment. Auriellen trade was significantly damaged, and MEG placed an embargo on Auriellen industry unless they privatized the Volucris Corsius Terrestri Teplam Altum.

In a bid to regain access to global markets, the Auriellen government held a conference disproving and condemning the MEG's accusations. The MEG, seeing the opening of an opportunity to decisively obtain control of Aurielle proper, drafted a false flag operation with the collaboration of Antlia and the Confederation of Level 154. A list of ultimatums was listed, which, if declined, would constitute the beginning of the incident. As Aurielle began the arms conference, it quickly became apparent that they would neither accept nor negotiate a settlement with the MEG. The Impresarios allowed for the AAC incident to play out; two soldiers bearing the AGF emblem and uniform walked into the conference and shot two MEG representatives; one Confederation of Level 154 and one [[The Anti-Entity Group|A.E.A.]] representative were accidentally hit and later succumbed to their injuries. Responding guardsmen fired at the two soldiers, killing them, and the conference came to an abrupt end.

The populations of the Confederation and MEG, enraged by the apparent showcase of Auriellen aggression, prepared for war, which would arrive two months after the incident. Antlian forces, assisted by a MEG contingent, attacked the town of Navarro, kickstarting the Lustruger Crescen War. Two weeks in, as Auriellen forces began an offensive against Antlia, MEG and the Confederation of Level 154 formally declared war and sent troops to fight the Auriellens. Aurielle would collapse in one month, overwhelmed by the combined armies of the coalition. Aurielle would be divided into three occupation zones starting in 1990 and until the beginning of the Korthie Casiagen War in 2000.

Remnants of the Auriellen government and military fled to the Backroom Colonists, where they sought shelter in diplomatic and military-protected safehouses. Following negotiations with the colonists, the Special Administrative Colony of Geminia was formed in 1991 and was catalyzed as the primary logistical hub and safe haven for the Auriellen insurgency. Solcito, having been overcome with grief due to his apparent failure to protect the Republic, appointed Nurem Pacen, the former vice president, as leader of Geminia. Under Pacen, Geminia began a period of lengthy mobilization and preparation for a fight for the liberation of Aurielle, assisted by his allies such as the Colonists and Arvans.

Meanwhile, the imposition of the Lowland and Highland system, the Open Border Policy, and the surveillance system found in the MEG, Antlian, and Confederation occupation zones, respectively, hastened the process of anti-imperialist resistance and a state of near-perpetual civil war by 1992. The Steppe Insurgency in [[Level 339]] and [[Level 340]], led by the Auriellen National Liberation Army and Republican Army of Volucris, was the largest continuous guerrilla campaign in the occupation period, followed by the Surian Uprising and Cinuevo Offensive.
{{Simple Image Block
|image=Hi!!.png
|caption=Air support during the Leosaros Offensive.}}
On February 12, 2000, Geminia invaded occupied Aurielle to regain control. In response, the Confederation, MEG, and Antlia formed a coalition (UKOPCOM) with the goal of counteracting Auriellen operations and pacifying the local populace. The colonists also began to train and assist the Auriellens through the Branch Auriga in 2001, similarly to Cascada and Arva, which sent their own expeditionary forces. The number of UKOPCOM troops present in Aurielle at its peak was roughly 65,000+, dropping to a meager 2,000 by 2018. UKOPCOM Secretary Ayden Morgan announced the coalition's surrender and withdrawal on March 22nd, 2019, after the wildly successful Caranda Offensive and the [[Level 149|nuclear infection detonation in Level 149]]. By the end of the war, Antlia was conquered, and various confederations of Level 154 and MEG territories were taken over.

Despite being focused on fighting the Korthie Casiagen War, many Auriellen commanders engaged in military operations outside of it, mainly the Auriellen intervention in the SNC-Cascadan War and Niwereld-Hezikian War, especially in their later stages when it had become evident that MEG could no longer sustain their operations in Aurielle. These Patoichist actions propelled the Auriellen state's international image as having the capability to fight various wars at once. Additionally, the principles of Aurielo Patoichism became enshrined in the 2019 constitution and education system.

Pacen's final actions before his resignation were the elimination of Solcito's Ant Laws, the endowment of equality for all, and increasing cooperation between Aurielle and its allies, deepening trade relations and promoting cultural exchanges. Aurielle stipulated a treaty of alliance and mutual assistance in line with the formulation of the House Dog foreign policy doctrine set out in 2019 that promoted international solidarity against actions they saw as colonial aggression. These talks resulted in the Volucris agreement in early 2020, which shaped the Volucris Strategic Economic and Security Accord (VSESA).
{{Simple Image Block
|image=AGF dudes.png
|caption=Auriellen Ground Forces troops patrolling around Mt. Antecia.
|align=left}}
In 2020, Arenet Matlon was elected as Aurielle's new president, the first since the 1985 election. Matlon's administration began a process of globalization as well as opened up diplomatic dialogue with various other nations to expand VSESA's member count and roles. The accord was divided into four levels per administrative changes: the Central Economic Union, the Common Political Chamber, the Mutual Defense Treaty, and the Military Response Contingent to allow for greater flexibility. Furthermore, with the establishment of the Autonomous Administrative Republic of Antlia and the Administrative Republic of Sirilis, Matlon drafted the Rollback Doctrine, seeing the Republic actively committed to the containment and rollback of MEG's sphere of influence.

After Aurielle completed the reconstruction that had been ongoing since 2013, with assistance from the Backroom Colonists, Veridia, Cascada, Hadrina, Branuba, Neshatra, Daxia, the [[Backrooms Red Cross]], and Arva, they were able to redirect additional funds toward their industries. Kaden Savaleo, the Minister of Finance, led a broad initiative focused on export-led economic growth. Auriellen peacekeeping missions, after the signing of the Uneas et Pacen Laupe Enacta, exemplified Auriellen dominance, most prevalently seen in the [[Cascadan Conflict]], which is often seen as the full solidification of the Auriellen superpower. Aurielle and VSESA have also become increasingly focused on combating international terrorist organizations such as [[Atrego]] and ZETA, as seen by Operation Noviscu Talon, Uppdragssköld, and Evolutionary Resolve.

The discovery of the Mẽrheeldrnay, Mokhacadtie, and Igtezdotejo sapient species during a VSESAN expedition has raised questions about expanding human rights to include most, if not all, sapient species that pose no danger to humans. This development also invites consideration of their inclusion in international politics, particularly following Uzequyut'inugurñe's entry into VSESA in 2021.

==Politics==
Aurielle is a federal presidential republic operating under a representative, democratic, and federalist system following ideas enshrined by Aurielo Patoichism, as established by the 2019 constitution. The president serves as the head of the government and state, envisioned as the upholder of peace and democracy against authoritarian or extremist regimes. Aurielle is composed of three constitutionally established levels of government: the federal government, the district governments, and the municipal offices. Groups enveloped by Auriellen territory are referred to as "Stantops" and are mostly self-governing and act as independent states but are, nonetheless, heavily influenced by the nation and are subjected to its laws and acts.

Suffrage is universal for all citizens 16 and older. National elections for the executive and legislative branches take place every five years; elections at the district and municipal levels depend on the district's laws but are limited to a seven-year period maximum per federal regulations.

===Federal Government===
The executive power of the Republic is vested within the president, who constitutes the head of state and the government and the commander-in-chief of the Auriellen Armed Forces when a state of defense is declared by Congress. The president is directly elected through a two-round system and serves under a two-term limit as mandated by Article 24 of the constitution; he executes, endorses, and enforces the law and has the ability to veto bills. He also appoints his cabinet, the 13 Constitutional Court seats, with Senate approval, and other offices.

Congress, or the Congress of Aurielle, is the federal legislative body of Aurielle and has operated in its current bicameral form since the formation of Aurielle in 1975, adopted from Arisa and Alioth's parliamentary system. It consists of two chambers, the Districtal Representatives of the Assembly (Praseavos Distrumiet di dem Assembleu) and the Federal Senate (Senat Fiuderale). Congress drafts federal law; declares war; imposes taxes; approves the national budget, although this is often reserved to the Federael Senate; and approves international treaties, ratifies diplomatic appointments, and has the power to impeach the President.
{{Simple Image Block
|image=Emty 2.png
|caption=Empty picture of the insides of the congressional building.}}
Constitutionally, legislative authority is centralized, but in action it is different. Internal matters concerning culture, society, and welfare are often routed to the Districtal Representatives of the Assembly, while foreign politics and economic policy are assigned to the Federal Senate. Regardless of this, these barriers are not superimposed by law and are normally broken, especially regarding economic policy. The Districtal Representatives of the Assembly have 224 seats, elected by a mixed-member representation. The Federal Senate, which comprises 114 senators. Of these 114, 92 are elected through a state-based representation through a single transferable vote, while 22 are elected through a closed party list.

Legislature voting is based on "rewostos," which function as the primary area to provide residents with representation in a legislative body. Their modern borders were standardized in 2022, with limited later adjustments following territorial expansion. There is no authority at this level, as it was made to not complicate the voting process; representatives and senators represent each specific rewosto.

The judicial branch of Aurielle is known as the Judiciary, which is overseen by the Ministry of Justice. The highest organ of the judicial branch is the Constitutional Court, which operates independently from the Ministry of Justice. As the highest court of Aurielle, the Constitutional Court ensures the correct application of law in the inferior and appellate courts and resolves disputes as to which lower court (penal, civil, administrative, or military) has jurisdiction to hear a given case. The Constitutional Court can reject, or confirm, a sentence from a lower court. If it rejects the sentence, it can order the lower court to amend the trial and sentencing, or it can annul the previous sentence altogether. The Constitutional Court is composed of 13 judges who serve 15 years total: 5 appointed by Congress, and the rest appointed by the president with Senate approval.

===Administrative divisons===
Aurielle is administratively organized on three levels. At the official highest level are the district-level-fixed federal units that exercise their own autonomy through an elected governor. Districts conform to rewostos, with borders standardized in 2022. While their roles are primarily designated for legislative elections, their actual function is to distribute authority between a collective of district governors and the independent council of a dominant district. As previously stated, each level inhabited by Aurielle is organized into its own district, with the exception of [[Level 339]] and any arid, or desert, level, which have three distinct districts and a mega-district, respectively.<ref>The three districts in Level 339 are known as Levom, Voriolia, and Dastia. The massive district is known as Resalia.</ref>

Below the districts are municipalities, the number of which are usually dictated by the districts' independent councils and are, usually speaking, artificially outlined because most Backrooms levels do not possess geographical characteristics. The emergence of "nasemos" has occurred more recently. These are regional units for economic and cultural administration, established following the 2022 Internal Reorganization Act of the Republic's Territories. They are based on both cultural and economic activity, marked primarily by the division of industrialized and rural activity.

===Administrative states===
At the end of the Korthie Casiagen War, the boundaries of the Republic had far extended beyond its pre-war borders, having occupied the entirety of Antlia and parts of MEG and the Confederation of Level 154. Antlia fell under the Antlian Military Occupation Zone, and the territories of both the Confederation and MEG were organized into the Reorganized Military Zone of Kaimana Proper.

The acts of violence against Auriellen occupant soldiers succeeded the Afakah Agreement, which began talks for the organization of an independent Antlia and Sirilis with collaborationist, unnegotiable policies enshrined in their constitution. On March 17th of 2020, a proposal by Zach Harper constituted the formation of a third territory, known as Laencaster, that separated Confederation and MEG occupation zones, but it was denied. However, what came of it was the proposition of the two states that virtually divided Sirilis into two confederal governments under a semi-presidential system. Following its passage, and amid ongoing sectarian violence, the two states that emerged from the Auriellen military occupation zones became recognized internationally as Antlia and Sirilis<ref>Kaimana Proper, the former occupation zone in Confedertion and MEG land, was eventually renamed to Sirilis after the territories in the Sires-Nilius line</ref> after the passing of the Terretatem Redorganisio Enacta.<ref> Territorial Reorganization Act</ref>

The ''Autonomous Administrative Republic of Antlia'' (AARA), under Marad Javaka, and the ''Administrative Republic of Sirilis'' (ARS), under Fletcher Hudson, are both puppet states that, by their 2020 constitutions, are bound to Aurielle in aspects of the economy and military. The Antlian Monos and the Sirilis Denarus are pegged to the Nolic; they are a part of the Volucris Strategic Economic and Security Accord's closest inner circle, and both military equipment and army organization are based on Aurielle's.

===Clients and Volucris Strategic Economic and Security Accord===
{{Simple Image Block
|image=Coalition Flag.png
|caption=Flag of VSESA.}}

The '''Volucris Strategic Economic and Security Accord''' (Volucris Strateque Oeconemicus et Securitate Acordus) (VSESA, colloquial; VESA; VSOSA in Aurielleno) is an intergovernmental political, economic, and international defense alliance divided into four chambers with a combined member count of up to 85 nations. Founded in the aftermath of the Korthie Casiagen War and primarily influenced, dominated, and often seen as denoting the boundaries of the Auriellen sphere of influence, it serves as a balance of power or counterweight to the MEG and its sphere. VSESA's enlargement, both through interventionist and non-interventionist means, has both been seen by MEG as a threat and established it as a major international organization. 

The organization consists of four chambers: the outer circle, the central circle, the central-inner circle, and the inner circle. VSESA's Central Economic Union (VSESA-CEU) is the economic chamber of the organization, aimed at establishing a single internal market and maintaining common policies on trade, agriculture, fisheries, and regional development; solving economic or trade conflicts; and sharing a common currency: the Nolic. Following is VSESA's Common Political Chamber (VSESA-CPC), which establishes a common unified policy in external relations and opens its members to a diplomatic consultatory with missions throughout the Backrooms. VSESA's Mutual Defense Treaty (VSESA-MDT) is the collective security system wherein its independent member states agree to defend each other against attacks by third parties as agreed under the Treaty of Hasholt; it comprises 23 nations, with the most recent being the [[Duchy of Cascada]], who joined the treaty in 2020 after the Cascadan Conflict. Lastly, VSESA's Military Response Contingent (VSESA-MRC) is only composed of Aurielle, Sirilis, and Antlia. Its members agree to participate in any conflict regardless of if it's defensive or offensive, unless stated otherwise.

The most notable member of VSESA is the Republic of Aurielle, with other important member states including the Duchy of Cascada, the Republic of Veridia, the Federation of Hadrina, the Union of Branuba, the National Federation of Neshatra, and the State of Arva. The comprising states of VSESA are often considered Auriellen client states, whose system has been criticized as a perpetuation of imperialist rhetoric under the guise of protection.

===Political parties===
Electoral politics in Aurielle operate under a multi-party system that has had a history of abrupt change. In modern times, the EFN, PU, and EFU have established themselves as the primary and dominant parties in both the presidential and legislative elections, but the EWSFA remains strong in the congressional nomination.
