===Elections===
Elections in Aurielle are held for several functions of the government: presidential elections (national), parliamentary elections (national), district governors (local), municipal mayor (local), districtal council members (local).

===Financing===
Political parties are able to use donations, dues, membership fees, and other forms of grassroots funding (plutocratic funding is also relatively common, but it depends on the party) to finance their campaigns. Parties participating in political elections are also eligible to receive funding from the Administration of Taxation and State Revenue (ATSR), typically 0.02% of the general budget revenues annually, after passing a set of regulations to avoid the system's exploitation.

Political parties are also allowed to receive aid or donations from foreign groups, albeit with strict restrictions, such as a 65,000 Nolic maximum and a limit that reserves it only to the political allies of the Republic. There have also been reported instances of them being funded by domestic corporations, including incidents of attempted meddling by the MEG through the usage of decoy enterprises and corporate entities that fund favorable parties, but they have mostly been cracked down on by the ASCIC.

==Military==
{{Simple Image Block|
|image=Package here.png
|caption=Two Auriellen [[Ocelot 1|Ocelot 1A2-S]] moving through Messanion area during the Caradan Offensive.}}
The Auriellen Republic has boasted one of the most powerful militaries amongst the faculty of nations in the Backrooms ever since its solidification as a great power in the 2020s. The formation of the contemporary Auriellen military has its roots in the unification of the Sidusan alliance's respective militaries into one unified force as part of the Common Unity Act. Despite this, it dates back to a long military history chronicling the Ciran Wars, the Alsanium Uprising, the Muran Conflict, the Vistokan Wars, the Antlian Revolution, the Netter Linsho War, and the most significant Korthie Casiagen War, along with other post-Korthie Casiagen War conflicts.

The Auriellen Armed Forces (Aurielleno: Corsius Armata Aurielleno; CAA, AAF) are the military forces of the Republic of Aurielle and are collectively formed of four branches: the Auriellen Ground Forces (AGF), the General Aerial Operations Command (GAOC), the Auriellen Maritime and Coastal Operations Force (AMCOF), and the Auriellen Security Council Special Forces (ASCSF). They are accompanied by two arms of services: the Auriellen Territorial Defense Force (ATDF), and the Naval Assault Brigade (NAB), belonging to AMCOF.
<br>It employs a professional, all-volunteer system where, under AFD Enacta 13/02/20, Est. 44, enlistment age is 15 and above. Aurielle's cultural militarism has catalyzed the military as one of the most common employment options. Each military branch has a domain and role, as determined by the Causco Adpaticius Corsius. The AGF conducts land operations; GAOC conducts air operations; ASCSF conducts special operations; AMCOF conducts maritime and littoral operations; NAB specializes in amphibious and maritime littoral operations primarily for supporting AMCOF; and ATGDF specializes in domestic operations, border guarding, and settlement defense and also serves as Aurielle's main law enforcement agency and gendarmerie force.

Auriellen military equipment is primarily manufactured domestically in various ordnance factories located throughout the Backrooms, all operated by Aurielle. Since they form a sequence of factories working in tandem, although still semi-independently from one another, it is often nicknamed the "Auriellen Ordnance Chain" (Cateos di Odeltum Aurielleno). Imported equipment and weapons often fill the rank wherever there is a shortage of weapons. The Auriellen ammunition industry is the largest ammunition producer in the Backrooms and is centered around the Volucris Corsius Terrestri Teplam Altum in [[Level 339]], which produces over 90% of the country's projectiles and bullets. Despite its usefulness, Aurielle has no mechanical control over the facility, and it often produces calibers that are unused or incompatible with Auriellen weaponry; although some are sold to other groups, a majority are practically useless and are either disassembled for their materials or discarded.

The Auriellen Ground Forces is the largest branch of the Auriellen Armed Forces and is commonly seen in training exercises with other VSESA and VSESA-aligned nations, instructing and assisting the ranks. The AGF has no primary service weapon, rather using an umbrella of weapons with a caliber of 7.62×51 mm NATO or 5.56×45 mm NATO, although it can include others under specific conditions. Per the Federal Ministry of Defense's Armed Forces Logistical Directorate, the Weaponry Chain System (WCS) functions in four levels: first command, mainland command, reserve command, and bottom command. The bulk of the AGF lies in the mainland command, and the de facto service weapon is the HK33, followed by the popular options of the G3A3/A4, FN FAL, M4, M1A, M16A2, and the Auriellen manufactured [[BRC-TZ]]. Other parts of the AGF, such as its logistic companies and its armored companies, are given reserve to bottom command weaponry.

The General Aerial Operations Command is heavily centered around helicopters and its airborne component. Outside of it, it is reserved for a limited count of reconnaissance and some attack drones. GAOC's air assault units utilize the same weaponry as the AGF, with the exception of the BRC-TZ, which serves as their de facto primary service rifle. Pilots typically carry only submachine guns at most, and often only sidearms, or have no weapons at all.
{{Simple Image Block|
|image=Walking.png
|caption=Members of the 48th Infantry Company marching into Covenna.
|align=left}}
AMCOF is the smallest-sized component of the army and is concentrated in Camp Tolelman's Port Lausadus, where they hold the nation's only blue-water-capable fleet composed of gunboats and three "mini-corvettes," although rumors of its decommission have spread around. AMCOF mainly holds regional brown-water fleets composed of improvised transport ships and assault boats to move troops from rivers and is also used limitedly for coastal defenses. AMCOF's Naval Assault Brigades are deployed as the main combat force in wetland and swampy levels.

The Federal Ministry of Defense (FMD) is the top-level federal agency, headed by the Federal Minister of Defense, responsible for military defense and the armed forces. The commander-in-chief per the Auriellen constitution is the federal minister of defense, who, with supervision from the president and the federal Senate, appoints the general staff of the armed forces. Article 122b-Cor decrees that in the state of defense, declared by Congress, the command in chief passes to the President. The FMD administers the AGF, GAOC, and AMCOF, while the ATDF and ASCSF are administered by the Ministry of Internal Affairs and Auriellen Security Council, respectively. Additionally, the president holds power over the FMD to form military policy; however, the FMD may request a congressional revision to repeal or change it.

Since the passing of the Uneas et Pacen Laupe Enacta<ref>Unity and Peace Bill Act.</ref> in 2020, in conjunction with the implementation of the Rollback Doctrine, Aurielle has played an active and significant role in peacekeeping operations and internation intervention. The Auriellen Armed Forces, and in turn, military policy, operates under a variation of the [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Just_war_theory just war theory], used to justify the Republic's military actions. As part of politican Quesca Scutionu's War & Insurgency Theory, the armed forces work to maintain and avoid any unnecessary collateral damage to win the hearts and minds of local and assist with integrating client states.

Aurielle's armed forces manage the largest defense budget in the Wake and are considered to have the strongest military as well. It has significant capabilities in both defense and power projection roles and is widely considered a pioneer in the implementation of untested technology, notably the deployment of drones in warfare; the domestication of entities for both non-combat and combat roles, as seen by [[Entity 107|Corasil Sinos Arma]] and [[Entity 90|Legoms Iagaselos]]; and the first use of [[Level 149|weapons of mass destruction by a group in the Backrooms]]. Federal reviews are common for the military, and any shortcomings in equipment are addressed under the Urgent Necessary Operational Military Requirements (UNOMR). Most of Aurielle's equipment is sourced from [[Entity 25|Counter Entities]].

==Economy==
The Auriellen economy operates under a system of mixed economy and so-called "fiscal federalism" (based on the Neshatra's idea of Pińondzwe Ałtonstatk), in which nasemos have the ability to manage their own economic policies. The federal government plays an important role in the domestic economic affairs through dirisme and price gouging. Aurielle's economy is among the largest in terms of nominal GDP (94,888,500 ₦; 56,933,100 USD) and performs well in healthcare and education in large urban centers.

Aurielle adheres to a free trade policy in part due to the globalist principles of Matlon and VSESA-CEU. Despite its policies favoring free trade between its allies, the government nonetheless prioritizes self-sufficiency in essential goods so that, in the event of a sudden halt in trade, the country would not face starvation or shortages of basic goods or industries. The agricultural and healthcare industries are subsidized by the government, while the energy, transportation, and defense industries are state-owned. Aurielle enforces anti-monopoly laws and implements anti-saturation laws to prevent excessive production of identical goods under different brands. The Republic's market has been catalyzed by a mix of small and medium-sized enterprises alongside large multinational corporations in its economy, which allows it to fill both niche and general market sectors. Volucris and Pastamill serve as Aurielle's largest financial centers.

The meat and agricultural sectors are exceptionally competitive markets within Aurielle. The Republic is predominantly considered the largest exporter of animal meat and agricultural products, of which its main ones are wheat, tomato, lettuce, and fresh produce. The nation does not have a main agricultural hub, rather encompassing many agrarian towns in the Levom District.

The electronics and manufacturing industries are enormous in Aurielle, especially in arms manufacturing, which is largely state-owned. The thriving industry and the adjacent cultural high ground placed on Auriellen military products have fostered uncertainty by foreign corporations when it comes to entering the Auriellen manufacturing market; many who have attempted it have failed, although some have succeeded. Additionally, the manufacturing market has faced continuous hardships and accusations, specifically of lobbying. Prospero and Vallis Descando serve as the industrial heartlands of Aurielle.

Aurielle is a member of various intergovernmental economic forums, of which the VSESA's Central Economic Union and the International Development 7 (ID7) represent its two most prominent memberships. Aurielle's largest trade partners are Antlia, Sirilis, Cascada, Burgunde, Branuba, Telaso, and Mordillo, most of them having free trade agreements with it, alongside other groups. Food, beverages, machinery, weapons, and minerals are the largest exports, followed by natural gas, which, in conjunction with minerals, is coagulated in the Resalian District. Aurielle imports machinery parts, pharmaceuticals, and chemicals. Tourism has also contributed to the Auriellen economic sphere, having seen rapid increase by the end of the Korthie-Casiagen War.

Transportation has also been a rising industry ever since the post-Korthie Casiagen War. Driven by the set of complex logistical routes built by the military to feed war efforts, Aurielle's transportation system is fully state-owned and focused on two systems: an extensive road system, primarily used by the military and emerging from preexisting roads within levels, and small rail systems connecting the largest population centers. In Aurielle, as is common throughout the Backrooms, cycling serves as the primary mode of transportation. Consequently, there are dedicated cycle tracks, typically located beside the roads. It is, however, not the only available form of transportation present; following the passing of the Bajeseas-Mestrewor Initiative, a form of bus became available in major population centers. Using decommissioned vehicles refurbished for civilian use, it is virtually the same as bus services, functioning along a route, calling at agreed bus stops according to a published public transport timetable.

The national currency of Aurielle is the Nolic, which is listed among the Backroom's most used currencies due to its implementation in members of VSESA's Central Economic Union. The exchange rate of the Auriellen Nolic (₦) is estimated to be 1₦ per 0.60 USD. The average income tax rate for Aurielle is 20% and 45% for the wealthy. Circumvention is reprimanded both culturally and legally, as it is seen as "an attempt to avoid the civic duties of the individual" and can bring upon extralegal punishments through vigilante justice, which is often tolerated in such cases. Each nasemo has the ability to set its own minimum wage, though by law they must be at or above ₦ 12.50 according to the 1982 Minimum Wage Increasement Act, launched to increase productivity.

===Hydrocarbon industry===
The hydrocarbon industry has been considered a large sector within Aurielle's economic output since its formation, primarily attributed to its oil-intensive combined warfare doctrines. Aurielle possesses one of the largest oil reserves in the Backrooms, of which 87% emerges from the Resalian district, followed by the Njeduan and Neasuran districts.

In 2013, the Auriellen administration under Nurem Pacen voted to nationalize its oil industry outright, effective 1 January 2016, with INO Pretos taking over and presiding over a number of holding companies. The vote was driven by various factors, but most importantly, feuds between the federal government and oil companies over a massive reduction of costs for upcoming military operations. In the subsequent years after nationalization, Aurielle built a vast network of refining and marketing systems in [[Level 340|Maraedus]] and [[Level 166|Diastoru]].

===Mining industry===
A vast and thriving mining industry operates within the Republic, being spearheaded by the Comiesteus Recdat, Corpus Atlas, and the RDA Natasawa. The beginning of the Caranda Offensive generated corporate and investment trust for the Republic, leading to the exponential growth of investors and foreign mining corporations operating within Auriellen land. There are currently an estimated 100+ mining licenses issued to foreign operators; operations are regulated and overseen by a concession system.

Additionally, there exists a specific niche within the mining industry mainly practiced in [[Level 15]] that consists of stripping the walls of their minerals for the acquisition of their composite materials. The niche's creation is directly linked to the production of military vehicles for the AGF and machinery. Mineral production is largely endowed by coal, copper, iron ore, lead, composite materials, and gold and is concentrated on [[Level 166]], [[Level 339]], and cave levels whose environments allow for large-scale mining operations.

===Agriculture===
Catalyzed as a forefront in Auriellen industry, the agricultural sector remains one of the biggest in the Republic. According to the latest agricultural censuses, there exist 34,000-42,000 farms, a vast majority of which are located in the Levom, Voriolia, and Dastiam Districts in Level 339. Of the total surface area in agricultural use (forestry excluded), grain fields take up 31%, vineyards 8.4%, apple orchards 5.2%, horticulture 2.8%, cherry orchards 1.7%, and the remainder is dedicated to other forms of orchards, pasture, and feed grains. Separate districts produce peculiar food products; Voriolia produces primarily maize corn, rice, grapes, meat, fruits, and dairy products, while Levom specializes in wheat, citrus and apricot fruits, and lettuce.

Aurielle is the leading producer of fruits, vegetables, and, most luxuriously, jam. Jam is often considered "the wine of the Auriellen" and is among the most popular and sought-after products of Aurielle, the most popular of which are the Rodosamnosea Plare and the Frutodo Foqueomes. The Auriellen agricultural sphere is one of the nation's most important aspects, as it contributes to its international image and influence.

==Culture==
{{Simple Image Block|
|image=Arepas.png
|caption=Different forms of Auriellen arepas alongside a Tamenan.}}
The Auriellens are culturally diverse peoples whose modern cultural formation came about in Halnora primarily through its constitution and was assisted by the Aurielleno language alongside war, which influenced local behaviors as the Auriellen administration expanded. The values of the Auriellen people are decentralized and vary in each nasemos. In Levom, the populace is deeply connected to nature due to the influence of Chekim Florism, valuing it and other facets, such as growth and hard work, as paramount, whereas in the Mehristea district, the concept of Conselevede values the minimization of inconveniences and harmonization. Despite this, certain values—specifically unity and patriotism—have become ingrained as "universal values" owing to their incorporation into the education system.

As a result, Auriellen culture is rather collectivist, although it tolerates individualism. Under this idea of social cohesion emerges the concept of "acta civejus," activities needed to continue the unity of the Auriellen state. The activities are categorized into "necessary" and "important." Necessary activities include paying taxes and engaging with the Auriellen cultural sphere, alongside others. In contrast, important activities encompass maintaining a respectable social life, participating in charitable efforts, and striving for personal excellence, all within the framework of competitive equality. This philosophy is encapsulated in the idea of "strive to mend, but bask in equality in the eyes of your competitor."

Military parades, acts of bravery during wartime, and serving the armed forces are the bulk of the fundamentals of the patriotic practices of Auriellen culture and are celebrated through gifts and cheers. War monuments and memorials, primarily from the Korthie Casiagen War, symbolize the bravery of the Auriellen people while also acknowledging the atrocities committed by occupying regimes against them.

Auriellens have an expansionist view of the Backrooms sourced from Orlan Sichel's philosophically anthropocentrist teachings in Deus et Peam, which express a general perception that the Backrooms are a means of God acknowledging humanity and thus a way to channel its unwavering desire to explore and learn. Sichel states that the Backrooms are one of the many dimensions created by God, which are accessed via his acknowledgment; under this pretext, it calls for humanity to conquer and expand its influence throughout the Wake, with its tribulations being but barriers set to test the species' capabilities. Sichel's teachings diverge in two primary interpretations: one coined by Recepeo Bunteva, and the other by Fuleo Bastere.

The interpretations disagree on two subjects: its purpose and the role of sapient species. Bunteva's faction asserts that the purpose of the dimension, and its ultimate goal, is to eliminate the barrier between God and man that was created by the first sin. It sees humanity as the one and only descendant of God and therefore views other sapient species as obstacles or temporary tools to achieve their purpose. Bastere's faction treats each dimension as a test of virtue that propels humanity forward and teaches it a valuable lesson. They view sapient entities as sons of gods rather than mere byproducts of their dimension's creations. These entities should be regarded as valuable allies whose existence aids in interpreting the lessons imparted by God.

===Cuisine===
Auriellen cuisine is typically versatile and hearty, having been historically influenced by the military. Auriellen foods are meant to be easily portable and can be eaten on the go; expensive dishes and those that are overly decorative are generally looked down upon as wasteful. The cultural traditions of ethnic groups and assimilation of other cultures, mainly of Latin American, Middle Eastern, and Mediterranean, have provided a highly diversified cuisine. Meals often involve and are based around meats, wheat-based foods such as bread, fruits, and vegetables.

Internationally, Auriellen cuisine is best known for the arepa, usually stuffed with shredded filling, sauce, and sometimes vegetables, eaten by Persan soldiers though later popularized; the caseum sandwich, a combination between a hunter sandwich and a burger; and the Aitor Bedalum, a sort of cookie filled with sweet fillings and then covered in flour, baking powder, salt, milk, eggs, and oil, then deep-fried, and then covered in cinnamon. In terms of beverages, Aurielle is particularly known for juices and the Tamenan, a derivation of chicha.

===Art===
In Aurielle, art is viewed as a deeply important medium of emotional and cultural expression. From the rise of Struvenism, the human condition and the struggle between man and its environment, with the inclusion of the idea of a hope for a better future, have been prominent themes present within Aurielle's artistic basis. Surrealism; Colominism, the art movement that reduces colors in paintings to elicit thought and reactions; and Noviscu Aurielle, the opposite of Colominism focused on vivid colors, reconstruction, and glory, encompass the overarching styles of contemporary Auriellen art.

===Internet Culture===
Aurielle oversees one of the digital sectors within the expansive Backrooms internet. This area, referred to as the "Auriellen sphere" or "Auriellen/VSESA internet," includes all of VSESA and nations aligned with it. The growth of this sphere is primarily driven by Auriellen military initiatives and the subsequent reconstruction efforts that often provide internet access to the affected countries.

These conditions fostered the present digital culture within the sphere, which is made up of ironic nationalism, and were catalyzed by a dark sense of humor that mocks regional stereotypes. Consequently, a branch of existentialist and ironic anecdotal humor that addresses atrocities from history has resulted in the emergence of memes satirizing it due to its "edginess" or focused on mocking events involving invaders or occupiers, mainly the MEG, such as the killings of Natan Wright and Harley Saunders.

==Demographics==
Aurielle is composed of various national groups but dominated by Auriellen mestizos, Mediterranean whites, Latinos, Arabs, and North Africans. According to government censuses, Aurielle's population is estimated at 76,554. A sizable portion of its citizens are Christian and live in its capital and largest city, Volucris. Minority religious communities, including Islamics, Pagans, Florists, and Kibestanists, also exist within the country. 70% of the population is native to Aurielle, while 12% are wanderers with no previous background, and the remaining are divided between 7.5% Antlians, 5% Sirilians, and 3.2% Unu Citedeans, and the remainder are composed of Branubans, Veridians, Istroyans, and Cascadans. The nation has a relatively high fertility rate; in 2020, it stood at around 4.3 per woman, and its population is young with a median age of 19.2.

Aurielle particularly stands out in its percentage of wanderers with no previous backgrounds, stemming from the Janus Enacta.<ref>Janus Act</ref> Passed by lawmarkers in 1981, it states that wanderers who are not affiliated with any group, are not tourists, and have clean records can acquire instantaneous citizenship once they interact with or pass by an Auriellen settlement, followed by a process of integration into Auriellen culture and sphere.

===Language===
Nearly all of Aurielle speaks [[Aurielleno]], which has served as the regional lingua franca for nearly a century. The only major ethnic groups that have retained their native tongues are the Antlians and Unu Citedeans, with Antlian and Untedean, respectively. Aurielleno is described as a Romance language with notable influence from other Romance languages, Latin, and some Chinese and English influences. Second-language proficiency is moderately widespread: approximately 34% of the population speaks Antlian as a second language, 16% speak English, and 7% speak [[Jinyi]].

===Education===
Aurielle enforces mandatory education for all children. The national education system has been in place since the 1960s, and virtually all citizens born since then have passed through it, with only a few exceptional cases, such as individuals with severe disabilities. Homeschooling and online schooling programs for rural settlements are available and focus on teaching children to a minimum of an eighth-grade level in fields like reading and writing and a sixth grade level in STEM fields. The country boasts a literacy rate of 79.5%. University education is entirely centralized on major population centers.

==Foreign Relations==
<tabber>
|-| MEG =
Relations between Aurielle and MEG are abysmal due to the MEG's exploitation of Aurielle, its invasion and 10-year occupation of the Republic's territory. The end of the Korthie Casiagen War saw rise of a Cold War-like division between the republican prorealist Aurielle against the monarchist postrealist MEG where both states compete for international influence over political dominance of the Backroom's geopolitical landscape.

Aurielle sought to strengthen bonds, most notably accomplished through the formation of VSESA; systematically isolate the MEG via foreign policy initiatives like the Rollback Doctrine; and expand Auriellen hegemony in the name of democracy. A détente between the two superpowers has been nearly unattainable, particularly during Matlon's tenure, because of insufficient diplomatic cooperation. Heightened tensions have repeatedly brought them to the brink of war, further deepening their divide.

|-| Backroom Colonists =
Due to continuous support from part of the [[Backroom Colonists]], both groups have strong positive relations with them, and they often do mutually beneficiary activities with each other.

Auriellens greatly appreciate the support of the Backroom Colonists during the Korthie Casiagen War, which included packages of food, supplies, and raw materials, and allowed them to form and incorporate Geminia post-war. Auriellen towns and cities often give gifts to coming Backroom Colonist members on missions for their service.

|-| The Anti-Entity Group =
Diplomatic relations between the AEG and Aurielle began when Halnora contracted the Anti-Entity Agency to clear an area where a base was planned to be constructed, with the Jahunas Contract. The historiography of AEG—Aurielle relations prior to the 2000s is considered relatively underdeveloped, with the first in-depth academic studies being published in the 2010s after various important developments between the two occurred during the Korthie Casiagen War. Today, diplomatic relations are complicated and have ongoing disagreements, but both groups see each other as strategic partners because of Auriellen political and military support after the Cascadan Conflict, their similar belief of human hegemony, and the relationship that followed.

During the Korthie Casiagen War, the AEG sporadically supported Aurielle in spite of distrust due to the AAC Incident that left an AEA representative dead; the 2016 Satam Disaster is considered the most substantial case of AEG involvement in the war, which served to improve relations. Post-Korthie Casiagen War relations were generally warm, with both groups working together to address entity issues, especially after [[Phenomenon 12|The Flicker]]. Aurielle-AEG relations became significantly strained due to the Cascadan War and the subsequent Auriellen military intervention in the conflict and the official denomination of [[Entity 107|Corasil Sinos Arma]] as a national symbol of Aurielle.

Following the signing of the Daveron Agreement, A-5 sough to improve relations with Aurielle. The two groups worked closely during the [[Cascadan Emergency]] and numerous other actions and operations. Aurielle provides the AEG with training, aid, and intelligence.

|-| Backrooms Investigation Foundation =
The [[Backrooms Investigation Foundation]] and Aurielle are strong, loyal allies. Both groups commonly back each other up, with Aurielle seeking their assistance in various peacekeeping operations in return for supplies and support. This, along with the fact that the B.I.F. provided intelligence and support to Aurielle during the Korthie Casiagen War, has led to positive terms between the two.

|-| Kauer Research Organization =
[[The Kauer Research Organization|Kauer]] and Auriellen relations are neutral; Rama Saom does engage in collaborations, but aside from that, relations are neither good nor bad.

|-| The System =
The System and Aurielle share close ties due to their militaristic society, often trading and carrying out joint military ventures.

|-| Ncipetheraz =
Ncipetherazi-Auriellen relations are quite unclear. This is due to the fact that the Ncipetherazi government utilises and publicises group relations with the Sylvainist Relations Model, as opposed to the traditional relations model that's composed of allies and enemies that you see used by Aurielle and most other groups.

With the traditional model, Auriellen and Ncipetherazi relations are neutral, and despite their polarizing beliefs—both culturally and ideologically—they somewhat frequently engage in trade and some form of cordialship, with both Ncipetherazi and Auriellen citizens being quite acceptive of eachother.

With the Sylvainist Relations Model—that only serves to count similarity in beliefs, as well as general populace consensus and many other factors—Aurielle is classified as "Against" due to the aforementioned polar-opposite beliefs. This is mostly because of Aurielles emphasis on cultural militarism and it's peacekeeping operations; 2 things that Ncipetheraz heavily disagrees with.

|-| Backrooms Association of the Red Cross =
The Republic of Aurielle maintains positive ties with the [[Backrooms Red Cross]]. Aurielle is part of the Red Cross' International Safety and Medical Committee (ISMC).

Aurielle boasts the ISMC's headquarters, which includes the usual meeting place of the Humanitarian Assembly in Volucris. The Republic of Aurielle is the third-largest provider of financial contributions to Red Cross missions. The nation and organization have a bilateral agreement that provides the Auriellen military the responsibility of protecting Red Cross facilities in exchange for healthcare and military and local medical/humanitarian assistance.

The main inspiration for Aurielle-Red Cross relations mainly rose during the aftermath of the Vistokan Wars, where the Red Cross took on a role of assisting initial Auriellen reconstruction efforts, especially those in LSA states. Complications arose during the Antlian riots and later on during the Antlian Revolution; however, the Red Cross led to the Lamsesjo ceasefire to evacuate civilians. Another step for contemporary Red Cross-Auriellen relations was during the Korthie Casiagen War, where the Red Cross was among the most pivotal organizations in the negotiation of ceasefires, evacuation of civilians, reconstruction of Aurielle, and mitigation of general suffering caused by the war.

|-| Duchy of Cascada =
The [[Duchy of Cascada]] and Republic of Aurielle enjoy close allyship, which began back all the way in Persa Uisan times. In 1926, a Persan expeditionary team discovered Level 107 and established a connection with one of the several local groups called Xiangzhou. Following initial contact, with fears of sanctions or interventions from emerging powers due to Persa's expansion, head figures in the fields of exploration led domestic movements that advocated for the opening of formal relations with the natives. Throughout the years, the groups maintained quiet, though relatively cordial, relations.

Xiangzhou maintained utmost neutrality during the various conflicts, though it engaged in friendly relations with emergent groups, such as Halnora, the Istauran Federation, and Arisa. With the foundation of Aurielle in 1975, Xiangzhou entered into a military alliance with the Republic, from which it would help establish the Duchy of Cascada, subsequently becoming the first client state for Aurielle under Sebastian's administration; coinciding with it came a purchase of Dem Edificeo for Rama Saom.

During the Korthie Casiagen War, Cascada supported Aurielle by supplying Auriellen forces with necessary materials such as weaponry and volunteers, a practice that would continue onwards. Postwar, the Republic awarded Cascada the status of "Eagle's Remige" and provided large economic and military compensations for their unwavering support throughout the Republic's history. In 2020, the onset of the Cascadan Conflict prompted Aurielle to begin small-scale interventions, preceding a full intervention following [[I.R.I.S]] attacks.

Cascada is currently one of the most pro-Auriellen groups, with 83% of Cascadans viewing Aurielle favorably, according to Backroom Colonists surveys, and 88% saying they trust Aurielle. 90% of Cascadans named Aurielle as their closest ally; meanwhile, 79% of Auriellens have favorable views on Cascada. Politically, Aurielle and Cascada have had firm and active political, economic, and military relationships. Auriellen government officials generally consider Cascada to be one of its closest allies and partners.

|-| Ruphis Proletarium =
Relations with the Ruphis Proletariums are severely strained due to the Proletarium's autocratic rule over its population and its exploitation of wanderers. During the Ruphis-Ncipetherazi War, Aurielle intervened under its own volition as its own side; however, Auriellen peacekeepers maintained close contact with Ncipetherazi forces, leading the Proletarium to label the Republic as a "sponsor of colonial aggression." The Proletarium also avoids mentioning Aurielle in its history books in fear of bolstering revolutionary sentiment among its citizens and youth.

Alleged Auriellen raids on Ruphisian forts to free Proles or citizens have further worsened relations. According to domestic polls and surveys, 62% of Auriellen citizens view an invasion for the deposition of the Sightseer as favorable.

|-| Confederation of Level 154 =
The Confederation of Level 154 is aggressive towards Aurielle due to the Korthie Casiagen War and alleged Auriellen involvement in the assassination of their Duke. Relations have improved since the Korthie Casiagen War, though they are still mostly hostile.

|-|Antlia =
Antlia-Auriellen relations have been complex throughout the years, though they have significantly improved since the establishment of the Administrative Autonomous Republic of Antlia in 2019. The Saggitani Revolution, one of the first major events between the Auriellen and Antlian governments, laid the groundwork for future conflicts and served as a major focal point in Sebastian Solti's anti-Antlian campaigns. Relations further worsened during the latter part of the Vistokan Wars, with the joining of Antlia into the LSA and the indiscriminate bombings committed by Sidus against Antlia and Libra.

After the Vistokan Wars, tensions between them and the rest of the Republic continued to rise, excluding the region from post-war reconstruction efforts. The government attempted to negotiate settlements between the Antlians and Auriellens, but all attempts failed, subsequently causing the Antlian Revolution. The revolution resulted in the election of Sebastian Solti, who imposed various anti-Antlian laws that became the set piece for the Netter Linsho War.

Antlian alignment and collaboration with MEG resulted in its acquisition of large amounts of Auriellen lands following the Lustruger Crescen War. The Open Land Policy, a policy that allowed the abrupt search of settlements within occupied Aurielle, caused several incidents and violent confrontations between local populations and occupants. The outbreak of the Korthie Casiagen War caused a surge of concentrated violence within Auriellen areas that Antlia occupied, which predated the widespread insurgency against the coalition. Throughout the conflict, local groups constantly resisted Antlia, causing it to be among the first powers to lose its holdings, and be the only one in the coalition to succumb to total annexation by the Auriellens.

After the war, the Auriellen congress passed the Terretatem Redorganisio Enacta, which mandated the creation of the Autonomous Administrative Republic of Antlia alongside the Administrative Republic of Sirilis. Particular prominence in the act pertained to Antlia, as many congressmen favored an agreement and alliance with the Antlians to stop further conflict, rather than occupation by fearmongering. Relations have since stabilized, with Auriellen presidents regularly touring Antlia and the average citizen of Antlia thinking neutrally or positively about the Republic.
